heading: Charge of property transferred to the Federal Government
label: 1301 Charge of property transferred to the Federal Government
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)130140:301.R.S. § 3750; , , .Pub. L. 89–30, § 279 Stat. 119June 2, 1965In subsection (a), the words “Except as provided in subsection (b)” are added for clarity.In subsection (b)(1), the words “the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 ( et seq.)” are substituted for “the internal-revenue laws” for clarity and for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the United States Code.26 U.S.C. 1
References in TextThe Internal Revenue Code of 1986, referred to in subsec. (b)(1), is classified to Title 26, Internal Revenue Code.

L. 89–30, § 279 Stat. 119June 2, 1965In subsection (a), the words “Except as provided in subsection (b)” are added for clarity.In subsection (b)(1), the words “the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 ( et seq.)” are substituted for “the internal-revenue laws” for clarity and for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the United States Code.26 U.S.C. 1

children.text: all land and other property which has been or may be assigned, set off, or conveyed to the Federal Government in payment of debts;
all trusts created for the use of the Government in payment of debts due the Government; and

children.label: A
children.text: assigned or set off to the Government in payment of debt; or

children.label: B
children.text: vested in the Government by mortgage or other security for the payment of debts.
real estate which has been or shall be assigned, set off, or conveyed to the Government in payment of debts arising under the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 ( et seq.); or26 U.S.C. 1
trusts created for the use of the Government in payment of debts arising under the Code and due the Government.
